title: Add Categories Management Page to Settings
story: As a product administrator, I want to manage categories (Line of Business and Topics) from the Settings section, so that I can organize and edit categorization options without leaving the administration area.
description: Add a Categories management page to the Settings/Sharing navigation. The page reuses the same spreadsheet-style editing experience used for Metrics Line of Business and Topics. Account owners can add, edit, and delete categories. A new permission controls whether shared users can also manage categories.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Categories' option appears in the left navigation under Settings
Account owner can view, add, edit, and delete categories from the Categories page
The Categories page uses the same spreadsheet-style UI as Metrics LOB/Topics
A permission flag controls whether shared users can manage categories
Changes to categories are reflected across the account for all shared users
ux_changes: New Categories page in the Settings section with spreadsheet-style CRUD interface. Left navigation updated with Categories link.
middleware_changes: Categories CRUD operations available from the settings context. New permission for category management added to the permissions system.
implementation_notes: Categories here refer to the Line of Business and Topics taxonomies used for organizing metrics, not WordPress-style post categories.
comment_summary: Developer requested clarification on what 'categories' meant. After a screenshot and explanation, implemented the spreadsheet-style categories page in the settings section.
